description: Scope domains every online-commerce initiative must address. Concern list mined from Standish CHAOS incomplete-requirements factors and commerce launch postmortems; briefs written from the inside view (Kahneman & Tversky 1979) reliably cover product and marketing while omitting money-flow and goods-flow domains.

concerns.seed_question: How does a sold item physically reach the buyer - who packs, who ships, at what cost, and with what delivery promise?

concerns.concern: Payment methods, gateway integration, and PCI obligations
concerns.prerequisite_note: A payment gateway account (with KYC/merchant onboarding lead time) must exist BEFORE the first sale can be taken; gateway onboarding is predecessor work, not launch-day work.
concerns.seed_question: Which payment methods are accepted at launch, through which gateway, and who carries the PCI scope?
What is the returns and refund policy, who pays return shipping, and what happens to returned stock?
How are fraudulent orders detected before shipment, and who absorbs chargeback losses?
Which channels bring buyers to the store, at what acquisition cost, and how is that measured?

concerns.concern: Sales tax / VAT registration and collection
concerns.prerequisite_note: VAT/sales-tax registration thresholds can require registration BEFORE the first cross-border sale (e.g. EU OSS). Verify against current jurisdiction.
concerns.seed_question: Where must the store register for VAT or sales tax before selling, and who files the returns?
How is stock tracked across channels, and what happens when an item sells out mid-order?
